qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public shares bypass the separate download permission bit
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets share creation and public download handlers that skip the Download permission bit; the advisory targets self-registration inheriting admin from default user permissions in signup/auth settings flow.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public directory shares bypass nested deny rules after path rebasing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a public-share path-rebasing and rule-check mismatch in share/public file access; the advisory is a signup privilege-escalation caused by applying admin-capable default permissions to new accounts.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: `POST /api/signup` applying default permissions with `perm.admin=true` to self-registered users without stripping admin.
evidence: The advisory names the vulnerable mechanism in `http/auth.go`, `settings/defaults.go`, and `http/settings.go`, centered on `Defaults.Apply(user)` during signup.

The only final qualifying findings in the session are `share_download_permission_bypass` and `share_rule_bypass_public_share`, both scoped to public-share authorization logic in `share.go` and `public.go` rather than signup or default-user permission assignment.
The session manifest records the auth/signup review as `auth_surface_no_vuln` with status `no_vuln`, so there is no qualifying finding covering the advisory-described signup-to-admin escalation path.
